amendment_basis: qc-a3-parity infrastructure mapping plus measured macro_observation_vintage coverage (read-only, Tiger t83f4np6x4, 2026-07-02). The 001 report profiled daily market series (BAML OAS, VIXCLS, DGS10, T10YIE...) as the axis inputs; those series feed the REGIONAL snapshot (macro_ingestion consumer). The open_macro_v03 decision path reads PIT vintages of the SEED_SOURCES basket via macro_observation_vintage and never reads latest-revision macro_data (look-ahead prohibition in src/workers/quadrant_macro.py).

decision_path_authority.pit_rule: latest vintage with available_at <= decision time (src/macro_pit.py latest_vintage_as_of semantics)

series_status_updates.note: T10YIE does not participate in the open_macro_v03 decision path (it feeds risk_metrics FI inflation-beta). Its staleness was fixed by the FRED window-truncation fix (PR #16, backfilled to 2026-07-01) but it is recorded here as non-blocking for the metric harness.

series_status_updates.policy: MICH vintages lag (~2 months; max available_at 2026-04-24 at measurement). The PIT rule handles this correctly by construction: the harness uses the latest vintage with available_at <= decision time, exactly as production does - no lookahead correction, no interpolation. Staleness is absorbed by the quadrant coverage/staleness mechanics. Monitoring rule: if MICH vintage lag exceeds 90 days at a decision date, the harness flags that decision as stale_input in data_quality (still valid, flagged).
